controls.rationale: Remove static AWS credentials from CI/CD and use auditable, job-scoped federation.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: AWS workflow jobs request id-token only when needed and use aws-actions/configure-aws-credentials to assume roles.

evidence.summary: Build-and-push requests id-token and assumes the image push role.
Deploy workflow uses OIDC role assumption instead of static keys.
Bootstrap stack defines GitHub OIDC IAM roles.

controls.title: GitHub Actions uses short-lived AWS credentials
controls.objective: Remove static AWS credentials from CI/CD and use auditable, job-scoped federation.

controls.rationale: Prevent other repositories from assuming this project's AWS roles.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: OIDC trust policies restrict the audience to sts.amazonaws.com and subject patterns to the noidilin/hiraya repository.

evidence.summary: GitHub OIDC role trust policies are declared in Terraform.
Tests validate expected OIDC role trust behavior.

controls.title: Repository-scoped OIDC trust
controls.objective: Prevent other repositories from assuming this project's AWS roles.

controls.rationale: Allow trusted pull requests to preview Terraform changes without modifying AWS resources or Terraform state.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: The plan role can read limited Terraform state and AWS describe/list/get APIs, publishes a plan artifact, and posts a sticky review comment.

evidence.summary: Trusted same-repository pull requests assume the plan role and publish the plan artifact.
Tests cover plan role state access limitations.
Script publishes plan context to the pull request.

controls.title: Terraform plan role is read-only
controls.objective: Allow trusted pull requests to preview Terraform changes without modifying AWS resources or Terraform state.

controls.rationale: Separate high-risk deploy and destroy actions from ordinary CI through branch restrictions and GitHub Environment approval.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Deploy and destroy workflows restrict execution to main, use the dev environment gate, generate a preflight plan, and then assume the apply role.
controls.gapSummary: No explicit gap recorded for the dev environment workflow.

evidence.summary: Deploy workflow uses main branch and environment approval before apply.
Destroy workflow requires explicit manual confirmation and environment approval.
Runbook documents controlled platform deployment.
Runbook documents controlled platform destruction.

controls.title: Infrastructure apply requires environment gate
controls.objective: Separate high-risk deploy and destroy actions from ordinary CI through branch restrictions and GitHub Environment approval.

controls.rationale: Build and publish immutable images only from trusted main-branch content and authorized repositories.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Image CI runs from main push or manual dispatch, uses Buildx linux/amd64, tags images with the commit SHA, assumes an OIDC image-push role, and publishes to authorized ECR repositories with immutable tags.

evidence.summary: Image pipeline detects changed services, builds images, and pushes via OIDC.
Service catalog defines allowed image repositories and manifest paths.
ECR repositories are managed by Terraform.
Image-push role trust is scoped in Terraform.

controls.title: Image build and publish trust boundary
controls.objective: Build and publish immutable images only from trusted main-branch content and authorized repositories.

controls.rationale: Detect high and critical CVEs before vulnerable images become trusted deployment inputs.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: ECR scan-on-push is enabled, tags are immutable, and image CI produces Trivy HIGH/CRITICAL reports.
controls.gapSummary: Trivy is currently advisory-only and does not fail the pipeline or require an exception approval for high-risk CVEs.

evidence.summary: Image pipeline runs Trivy and records scan output.
ECR repositories configure image scanning and tag behavior.
Rollback runbook supports response to bad service images.

controls.title: Container image vulnerability scanning
controls.objective: Detect high and critical CVEs before vulnerable images become trusted deployment inputs.

controls.rationale: Permit controlled image-version updates while avoiding unrelated repository writes or mixed infrastructure/application changes.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: The update-manifests job limits contents: write to the minimal job that rewrites GitOps image fields and commits back to main.
controls.gapSummary: Accepted risk: the checkout write token must persist for rebase and push, so the job remains a high-value credential exposure point.

evidence.summary: Only update-manifests needs contents: write and pushes manifest changes.
GitOps source is a narrow manifest tree.
Service catalog constrains manifest update targets.

controls.title: Manifest update write-token exposure is bounded
controls.objective: Permit controlled image-version updates while avoiding unrelated repository writes or mixed infrastructure/application changes.

controls.rationale: Reduce supply-chain drift from third-party GitHub Actions by pinning dependencies to immutable revisions.
controls.boundarySummary: Allow the named actors to perform the intended action only through the controlled workflow; deny bypasses, broad privileges, and unreviewed changes.
controls.implementedSummary: Major third-party actions are pinned to commit SHA revisions and checkout usually disables persisted credentials.

evidence.summary: Actions are referenced by commit SHA and checkout disables persisted credentials.
Image CI pins third-party actions and scopes credentials.
Deployment workflow pins actions.

controls.title: Third-party GitHub Actions are pinned
controls.objective: Reduce supply-chain drift from third-party GitHub Actions by pinning dependencies to immutable revisions.
